content.problemStatement:
Chef is celebrating his birthday today and has bought XXX **chocolates** to distribute among his classmates.
There are NNN **students** in his class.

Chef gives **exactly one chocolate to each student**, and **each student can receive at most one chocolate**.

Given that N≤XN \leq XN≤X, determine how many chocolates Chef will have **left at the end of the day**.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateRemainingChocolatescalculateRemainingChocolatescalculateRemainingChocolates – This function calculates the number of chocolates left after distributing one chocolate to each student.

Parameters
XXX : An integer representing the total number of chocolates Chef bought.
NNN : An integer representing the number of students in the class.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the **number of chocolates remaining** after distribution.
Constraints
1≤X≤1001 \leq X \leq 1001≤X≤100
1≤N≤1001 \leq N \leq 1001≤N≤100
X≥NX \geq NX≥N
Each student receives **exactly one chocolate**

Input Format

A single line contains two integers **X** and **N**.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the number of chocolates Chef has left.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and N, where X is the total number of chocolates Chef bought and N is the number of students (1 ≤ N ≤ X ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line — the number of chocolates remaining after giving one chocolate to each student.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 10 - 6 = 4 chocolates left.\nTest case 2: 5 - 5 = 0 chocolates left.\nTest case 3: 20 - 15 = 5 chocolates left.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply compute the difference X - N, because Chef gives exactly one chocolate to each of the N students and cannot give more than one to any student. The remaining chocolates are the surplus after this distribution. The algorithm iterates over all test cases, reads X and N, computes X - N, and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
idx = 1
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    X = int(data[idx]); N = int(data[idx + 1]); idx += 2
    out.append(str(X - N))
print('\n'.join(out))
